title: Iliad, Book 11: The Aristeia of Agamemnon
description: Book 11 of Homer's Iliad: The Aristeia of Agamemnon.
introduction: Book 11 of the Iliad: The Aristeia of Agamemnon. The Greek text is from the Venetus A manuscript (10th century), the oldest complete manuscript of the Iliad.

translation.original_text: Ἠὼς δ᾿ ἐκ λεχέων παρ᾿ ἀγαυοῦ Τιθωνοῖο ὄρνυθ᾿.
translation.text: Dawn rose from beside lordly Tithonus. Agamemnon armed himself and raged across the field, slaying Trojans until he was wounded. Then Hector pressed the attack, and the Greeks fell back. Odysseus was wounded, Ajax fought a rearguard action, and Achilles watched from his ship.
